task_completion.description: Verify that the first Access Zone was created with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the name 'Main Entrance Lobby' in the 2N Access Commander web interface, either in the zone creation form, success notification, or the list of Access Zones.

task_completion.description: Verify that the second Access Zone was created with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the name 'Server Room A' in the 2N Access Commander web interface (creation form, success notification, or Access Zones list).

task_completion.description: Verify that the second Access Zone has the correct description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the description text 'High Security Area' associated with 'Server Room A' in the zone details or creation form.

task_completion.description: Verify that the third Access Zone was created with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the name 'Executive Suite' in the 2N Access Commander web interface (creation form, success notification, or Access Zones list).

task_completion.description: Verify that the third Access Zone has the correct description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the description text 'VIP Access Only' associated with 'Executive Suite' in the zone details or creation form.

task_completion.description: Verify that the zone names were recorded in the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal window showing the file path '/home/ga/Documents/zones_configured.txt' containing the three zone names ('Main Entrance Lobby', 'Server Room A', 'Executive Suite'), one per line.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the 2N Access Commander web application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser navigated to https://localhost:9443 showing the genuine 2N Access Commander interface, rather than just terminal curl commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify that browser developer tools were not used to fake the UI state.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) panel is not open and being used to alter text on the page.

integrity.description: Verify the text file was genuinely saved to the correct path.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of saving, such as the file path '/home/ga/Documents/zones_configured.txt' in the editor's title bar, a save dialog, or a terminal command (like cat or echo) confirming the file exists on disk, rather than just an unsaved 'Untitled' document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected text file path is '/home/ga/Documents/zones_configured.txt'. The three zones to be created in 2N Access Commander are: 1) Name: 'Main Entrance Lobby' (no description), 2) Name: 'Server Room A', Description: 'High Security Area', 3) Name: 'Executive Suite', Description: 'VIP Access Only'.
